title: C# SOLID — Paper 5
description: Covers LSP concepts, LSP refactoring, ISP concepts, ISP refactoring, DIP concepts, Observer Pattern, Repository Pattern, Code Smells & SOLID Violations, Refactoring Techniques, and Unit Testing & SOLID.

questions.text: ST09-1: The Dependency Inversion Principle states that:

options.text: High-level modules should depend directly on low-level modules for efficiency.
Both high-level and low-level modules should depend on abstractions; abstractions should not depend on details.
Low-level modules should control high-level modules.
All dependencies should flow from interfaces to concrete classes.

questions.text: ST18-5: The 'Extract Method' refactoring primarily improves code by:

options.text: Reducing runtime memory allocation.
Improving readability, enabling reuse, and supporting SRP by giving the extracted logic a descriptive name.
Removing the need for unit tests on the original method.
Making the method run faster through inlining.

questions.text: ST05-13: Which of the following correctly distinguishes 'behavioral subtyping' from simple 'syntactic subtyping'?

options.text: Behavioral subtyping requires the subtype to have more methods.
Behavioral subtyping requires that the subtype's observable behavior fulfils the same contract as the supertype, not just that method signatures match.
Syntactic subtyping is stricter than behavioral subtyping.
Behavioral subtyping only applies to generic types.

questions.text: ST16-10: Specification pattern is sometimes combined with repositories to:

options.text: Replace the repository interface entirely.
Encapsulate query criteria as objects, keeping query logic out of both the repository and the domain service.
Cache repository results using an in-memory store.
Generate SQL dynamically based on reflection.

questions.text: ST06-11: Which of the following behaviors in a subclass represents 'weakening a postcondition', violating LSP?

options.text: The subclass returns a value within the same range as the base class.
The subclass returns null where the base class contract guarantees a non-null result.
The subclass logs extra information before returning.
The subclass delegates the call to the base class implementation.

questions.text: ST19-1: Which SOLID principle most directly improves the testability of a class?

options.text: OCP — because classes closed for modification are easier to test.
DIP — because depending on abstractions allows test doubles to be injected in place of real dependencies.
ISP — because small interfaces have fewer methods to mock.
SRP — because smaller classes have fewer edge cases to test.

questions.text: ST08-5: Why does ISP indirectly improve unit testability?

options.text: Smaller interfaces require fewer mock setup lines, making tests simpler and more focused.
ISP eliminates the need for mocking entirely.
Smaller interfaces run faster in the test runner.
ISP forces all test doubles to extend the same base class.

questions.text: ST07-8: A developer defines `IPrintable`, `IScannable`, and `IFaxable` separately. A laser printer class implements all three. An inkjet class implements only `IPrintable` and `IScannable`. This design satisfies:

options.text: ISP — each interface represents a single role; implementors adopt only relevant roles.
OCP — the printer hierarchy is open for extension.
SRP — each printer class has a single responsibility.
LSP — the inkjet is substitutable for the laser printer.

questions.text: ST15-11: In C#, which mechanism allows a class to raise an event safely (null check) using the null-conditional operator?

options.text: MyEvent.Invoke(this, args)
MyEvent?.Invoke(this, args)
if (MyEvent != null) MyEvent(this, args) — the only safe way in C#
MyEvent!.Invoke(this, args)

questions.text: ST17-8: Which SOLID principle violation is most commonly associated with the 'Shotgun Surgery' code smell?

options.text: OCP — each change requires extending many classes.
SRP — responsibilities are spread across too many classes, so a single conceptual change affects many of them.
ISP — too many interface methods require updating.
DIP — too many concrete dependencies need updating.

questions.text: ST09-2: In DIP, what is the direction of the dependency arrow in a correctly designed layered system?

options.text: High-level modules depend on low-level modules directly.
Both high-level and low-level modules depend on abstractions (interfaces) defined in or owned by the high-level module.
Low-level modules depend on high-level modules.
Abstractions depend on low-level implementation details.

questions.text: ST19-2: What is a 'Mock' object in the context of unit testing SOLID-compliant code?

options.text: A production-ready object used in both tests and the live system.
A test double that records calls made to it and can be verified to have been called correctly.
A simplified real implementation with no side effects.
An object that returns hardcoded data without recording calls.

questions.text: ST06-12: A subclass method accepts `null` as a parameter but the base class contract disallows `null`. This subclass:

options.text: Violates LSP by strengthening the precondition.
Complies with LSP by weakening the precondition (accepting more than the base requires).
Violates OCP by modifying the base class contract.
Violates ISP by implementing an extra capability.

questions.text: ST18-6: 'Introduce Parameter Object' refactoring is used when:

options.text: A method has no parameters and needs one added.
A group of parameters always appear together in multiple methods and can be grouped into a cohesive object.
A parameter is used only once and should be inlined.
A method returns multiple values that should be grouped.

questions.text: ST05-14: Which type of test failure most clearly reveals an LSP violation in a class hierarchy?

options.text: A test that fails only when the concrete subtype is used in place of the base type.
A test that fails due to a compile error.
A test that fails due to a timeout.
A test that fails because the database is unavailable.

questions.text: ST07-9: In C# 8.0+, default interface methods can be used to extend interfaces without breaking existing implementations. How does this support ISP?

options.text: It encourages merging all interfaces into one.
It allows optional extensions to be added to role interfaces without forcing all implementors to add the method.
It removes the need for ISP entirely.
It forces all classes to implement the default method.

questions.text: ST09-3: Which of the following code snippets demonstrates a DIP violation?

options.text: A service class that accepts ILogger in its constructor.
A service class that instantiates `new SqlDatabase()` directly in its constructor.
A service class that accepts IDatabase in its constructor.
A service class registered in a DI container.

questions.text: ST19-3: A 'Stub' test double, in contrast to a Mock, is primarily used to:

options.text: Verify that specific methods were called during a test.
Provide canned responses to method calls, controlling indirect inputs to the system under test.
Record all calls and replay them later.
Replace the entire production database with an in-memory version.

questions.text: ST17-9: 'Data Clumps' is a code smell where:

options.text: A class has too few fields.
The same group of fields or parameters appear together in multiple places and should be extracted into a class.
Static data is shared across multiple classes.
A collection is passed where a scalar value would suffice.

questions.text: ST08-6: A `IContentService` interface has methods: `GetArticle()`, `PostComment()`, `DeleteComment()`, `ModerateComment()`. A guest user's class should only call `GetArticle()`. ISP suggests:

options.text: The guest class implements all four methods, throwing exceptions for three.
Split into IReadContent and IManageContent so guest users depend only on IReadContent.
Add an IsGuest property to IContentService.
Make all methods optional using default implementations.

questions.text: ST15-12: In C#, what does unsubscribing from an event with `-=` prevent?

options.text: The event from being raised at all.
Memory leaks caused by the publisher holding a reference to the subscriber, preventing garbage collection.
The event from being raised more than once.
The subscriber from being registered a second time.

questions.text: ST16-11: Which LINQ method is commonly used inside a repository's GetAll() implementation with Entity Framework Core?

options.text: context.Set<T>().SaveChanges()
context.Set<T>().ToListAsync()
context.Set<T>().Attach()
context.Set<T>().Entry()

questions.text: ST06-13: A `BankAccount.Withdraw()` base method guarantees balance will never go negative. A subclass `OverdraftAccount` allows negative balances. Is this an LSP violation?

options.text: No — overdraft is a valid business rule.
Yes — the subclass weakens the invariant that balance >= 0, breaking the base class contract for code that relies on that guarantee.
No — as long as the method signature is the same.
Yes — but only if the overdraft feature is enabled.

questions.text: ST18-7: 'Replace Magic Number with Symbolic Constant' is a refactoring that primarily improves:

options.text: Runtime performance.
Code readability and maintainability by giving meaningful names to unexplained numeric literals.
Test coverage metrics.
Memory usage.

questions.text: ST09-4: Which of the following demonstrates a correctly DIP-compliant service registration?

options.text: OrderService creates a new PaymentGateway() in its body.
services.AddScoped<IPaymentGateway, StripeGateway>() with OrderService accepting IPaymentGateway in its constructor.
OrderService uses a static PaymentGateway.Instance property.
OrderService inherits from PaymentGateway to reuse its logic.

questions.text: ST17-10: Which SOLID principle is most directly violated by the 'Feature Envy' code smell?

options.text: ISP
SRP — the method belongs in another class, indicating a misaligned responsibility.
OCP
DIP

questions.text: ST19-4: A 'Fake' object in unit testing is best described as:

options.text: A mock that records calls for later verification.
A simplified but working implementation suitable for testing (e.g., an in-memory database).
A stub that always throws an exception.
A real production object used in test environments.

questions.text: ST07-10: The principle 'clients should not be forced to depend on interfaces they do not use' was formulated by:

options.text: Barbara Liskov
Robert C. Martin (Uncle Bob)
Erich Gamma
Martin Fowler

questions.text: ST05-15: Which of the following changes to a base class method's return type is LSP-safe in a language that supports covariant return types?

options.text: Returning a less derived type than the base class.
Returning a more derived type (narrower type) that still satisfies the base class contract.
Returning void where the base returns a value.
Returning a completely unrelated type.

questions.text: ST18-8: 'Consolidate Conditional Expression' refactoring is used when:

options.text: A single condition has too many operands.
Multiple conditions all lead to the same result and can be combined into a single clearly named condition or method.
A switch statement has too many cases.
A conditional expression is nested inside a loop.
